Impact: Iterates through authors and fetches books lazily in a loop triggering N additional DB queries.
Solution: Use @EntityGraph or JOIN FETCH in the repository.

Impact: If the book update fails, the author update is not rolled back, causing data inconsistency.
Uses a plain HashMap in a singleton bean; concurrent requests will cause crashes or data loss.
Solution: Use ConcurrentHashMap or an external cache (Redis).

Impact: Triggers lazy-loading of collections during hashCode/equals, causing StackOverflowError.
Solution: Use @Getter/@Setter and manually override equals/hashCode with the ID only.

Impact: Exposes full Java StackTrace to API clients in the response body.
Solution: Log stack trace internally; return only a generic message/Correlation ID.

Impact: Static HMAC secret in code is easily compromised if the source is leaked.
Solution: Use Asymmetric RSA keys and fetch from a JWKS URI.

Impact: Uses isAuthenticated() instead of role-based checks, allowing guests to edit data.
.permitAll() on legacy paths creates an unauthenticated "backdoor" to data.
Solution: Ensure all API paths require authentication unless explicitly public.

Impact: Disabling CSRF without justification is dangerous if the API uses session cookies.
Solution: Re-enable CSRF or justify its removal based on the client type.

Impact: H2 Console enabled without security limits allows direct DB manipulation.
Solution: Disable in production; restrict to Admin roles in development.

Impact: Returns Book entities instead of DTOs forcing the API to follow the DB schema.
Solution: Create BookDTO and map entities to DTOs in the service layer.

Impact: Missing @Valid and Jakarta constraints allows invalid data to enter the system.
Solution: Add @Valid to Controller and @NotBlank/@Min to DTO records.

Impact: External system URLs are hardcoded as strings instead of configuration.
Solution: Move to application.properties and use @Value or @ConfigurationProperties.

Impact: Local try-catch catches all errors and returns a vague string, hiding bugs.
Solution: Remove local try-catch; let GlobalExceptionHandler handle and log the error.

Impact: Returns 200 OK for all operations; uses @PatchMapping incorrectly.
Solution: Use correct status codes (201 204 404) and semantically correct HTTP methods.
